page: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.large is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The instance provides 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. There is no bundled local storage or GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with single-threaded gzip compression reaching 34.07 GB/sec and the Geekbench single-core score registering at 2164.0, both placing in the top 25 percent. However, multi-core performance is limited, as evidenced by a poor stress-ng multi-core CPU score in the bottom 10 percent. Memory bandwidth also shows constraints, with large-block read-only bandwidth measured at 20.61 GB/sec, placing it in the bottom 10 percent.

The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8) for matrix operations, and cryptographic acceleration (AES, VAES, SHA_NI). These hardware features make the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for entry-level compute workloads that benefit from modern instruction sets but do not require high memory capacity or multi-core scaling. Suitable workloads include single-threaded processing, text processing, lightweight web serving, and small-model text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs from 1 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C core running at 3.9 GHz. It is configured with 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory, utilizing the Nitro hypervisor on an x86_64 architecture. The server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Single-core benchmarks are strong, with high single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench single-core scores. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth performance are weak, reflecting its entry-level compute profile. The processor supports advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX), offering qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that leverage these instructions without requiring high memory density. It is best suited for single-threaded tasks, text processing, and lightweight web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 4.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance for entry-level workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.large is a compute-optimized instance with 2 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 4.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: An entry-level compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing, DDR5 memory, and advanced vector and matrix instruction sets.
bullet_points: Two dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor at 3.9 GHz
Four gigabytes of high-speed DDR5 memory at 7 GHz
Strong single-core performance in text processing and compression benchmarks
Hardware support for Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512
Best suited for single-threaded applications, text processing, and light web serving
